blocks.text: **09 Pronoun**

A word which is used in place of noun is called pronoun. Pronoun is used to avoid repetition of noun in a sentence.
वह शब्द जो noun के स्थान पर प्रयोग किया जाता हैं उसे pronoun कहते हैं।
Pronoun का प्रयोग noun के दोहराव को रोकने के लिए किया जाता है।
**TYPES OF PRONOUN**

items: Personal Pronoun
Reflexive Pronoun
Relative Pronoun
Distributive Pronoun
Demonstrative Pronoun
Reciprocal Pronoun
Interrogative Pronoun

blocks.text: Exam में इन्हीं 8 pronouns से questions पुछे जाते है।
**1. Personal Pronoun**
The pronoun which talks about persons is called personal pronoun.
**Important rules of personal pronoun**
**Note:-** Possessive pronoun के बाद noun का प्रयोग ना करे और Possessive adjective के बाद noun का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: __OUR__ friendship is great. (P.A.)
__OURS__ is great friendship. (P.P.)
__MY__ house is big. (P.A)
__MINE__ is a big house. (P.P)

blocks.text: As और than के बाद pronoun के subject form का प्रयोग करें यदि दो subjects के बीच comparison (तुलना) हो और as और than के बाद pronoun के object form का प्रयोग करें यदि दो objects के बीच comparison (तुलना) हो।

items.text: He __is better than__ I . [__He__ (S.F) ... __I__ (S.F)]
She is as cool as They . [__She__ (S.F) ... __They__ (S.F)]
He likes me more than her . [__me__ (O.F) ... __her__ (O.F)]

blocks.text:
इन Words के बाद हमेशा pronoun के object form का प्रयोग करें:-
let, like, except, but, separation, leave, favour, pardon, any preposition etc.

items.text: I like everybody except him.
Leave me alone.

blocks.text: It + be(form) + subject form of pronoun
It के बाद यदि be के किसी भी form का प्रयोग हो तो उसके बाद pronoun के subject form का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: It is I who have done it.
It was they who did it.

blocks.text: सामान्य या अच्छे sentences में singular pronouns को 231 के क्रम में लिखें।

items.text: You, he and I are students.
You, he and I have done it.

blocks.text:
**Note:-** 231 का अर्थ हैं पहले second person फिर third person और अंत में first person.
First person – I, we
Second person – you
Third person – He, she, They, It, Name etc.
Bad sentences (बुरे वाक्यों) में pronouns को 123 के क्रम

items.text: I, you and he are thieves.
I, you and he were making mistakes.

blocks.text: यदि sentence में सभी plural pronouns हो या singular और plural दोनों pronouns का प्रयोग हो तो सभी sentences में pronouns को 123 के क्रम में लिखे।

items.text: We, you and they are students.
We, you and they are robbers.

blocks.text: **2. Reflexive Pronoun**

जब Pronoun में self या selves को जोड़ा जाए तो उसे reflexive pronoun कहते है।
Singlular pronoun के साथ self जोड़ते हैं और plural pronoun के साथ selves जोड़ते है।

यदि इन **verbs** के बाद **object** ना हो तो उनके ठीक बाद **reflexive pronoun** का प्रयोग करें:-
Absent, present, introduce, adapt, adjust, surrender, acquit, avail, reconcile, enjoy, revenge, avenge, pride, resign, accutom etc.

items.text: I enjoyed myself in the party.
We must avail ourselves of this opportunity.

blocks.text: **Note:- 1** Passive voice में reflexive pronoun का प्रयोग ना करें।
**Note:- 2** यदि absent का प्रयोग adjective के रूप में हो तो उसके साथ reflexive pronoun का प्रयोग ना करें।

items.text: He was absent __absent__ . (Adjective)
He __absented__ himself from the school. (verb)

blocks.text: यदि absent के पहले be के किसी भी form का प्रयोग हो तो absent adjective होगा।
**Note:- 3** यदि surrender का अर्थ घूटने टेकना हो तो reflexive pronoun का प्रयोग करें लेकिन यदि surrender का अर्थ आत्मसमर्पण करना हो तो reflexive pronoun का प्रयोग ना करें।

items.text: He surrendered before the police. (आत्मसमर्पण)
He surrendered himself before his failure. (घूटने टेकना)

blocks.text: **Note:- 4** Pride का प्रयोग अगर verb के रूप में हो तो reflexive pronoun का प्रयोग करें लेकिन यदि pride का प्रयोग noun के रूप में हो तो reflexive pronoun का प्रयोग ना करें।

items.text: He __prides__ himself on his body. (verb)
He takes __prides__ in his body. (noun)

blocks.text: **Note:- 5** Accustom का प्रयोग यदि verb के रूप में हो तो reflexive pronoun का प्रयोग करें लेकिन यदि accustom का प्रयोग Adjective के रूप में हो तो reflexive pronoun का प्रयोग ना करें।

items.text: He __accustomed__ himself to the weather. (verb)
He got __accustomed__ to the weather. (Adjective)

blocks.text:
इन Verbs के साथ reflexive pronoun का प्रयोग ना करें:-
bathe, move, keep, turn, hide, rest, qualify, stop, shave, dress, undress, open, sell, concentrate, hurry, feel, wash etc.

items.text: Keep uptated.
He has qualified for the post.

blocks.text: Reflexive pronoun का प्रयोग subject या object के रूप में तब तक ना करें जब तक उससे related noun या pronoun का प्रयोग ना हो चुका हो।

items.text: Myself Ram here. (×)
I am Ram here. (✓)
I myself Ram here. (✓)

blocks.text: **3. Relative Pronoun**
ये sentences को जोड़ने का कार्य करते हैं। who, which, that etc. का प्रयोग relative pronoun के रूप में किया जाता है।
Adjective के superlative degree के बाद relative pronoun 'that' का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: He is __the best__ boy that I know. [S.D]

blocks.text:
इन Words या phrases के बाद relative pronoun 'that' का प्रयोग करें:-
All, everything, nothing, something, anything, the same, the few, the little, the only etc.

items.text: All that glitters is not gold.
He is the same man that helped me.

blocks.text: It के बाद यदि be की form हो और उसके बाद एक proper noun हो तो उसके साथ जरूरत के अनुसार who/which या that का प्रयोग कर सकते है।
It + be (form) + proper noun + who/which/that

items.text: It is Ram who helped me.
It is Delhi which I want to leave.

blocks.text: It के बाद यदि be का form हो और उसके बाद proper noun को छोड़कर कोई अन्य noun हो तो उसके बाद relative pronoun 'that' का प्रयोग करें।
It + be(form) + other noun except proper + that

items.text: It was milk that I wanted.
It is happiness that he wants.

blocks.text: **4. Distributive Pronoun**
Each, either, neither, any और none को distributive pronoun कहा जाता है।
**Each:-** Each का प्रयोग प्रत्येक के अर्थ में दो या दो से अधिक व्यक्तियों का वस्तुओं के लिए होता है।

items.text: Each boy has come.
Each of the boys has come.

blocks.text: **Either:-** Either का प्रयोग दो में से किसी एक के लिए होता है।

items.text: Either boy is coming.
Either of the men has done it.

blocks.text: **Any:-** Any का प्रयोग दो से अधिक में से किसी एक के लिए होता है।

items.text: Any of these five boys has come.
Any of you is able.

blocks.text: **Neither:-** Neither का प्रयोग दो में से एक के लिए भी नहीं होता है।

items.text: Neither boy is good.
Neither of you is bad.

blocks.text: **None:-** None का प्रयोग दो से अधिक में से किसी के लिए भी नहीं के लिए होता हैं।

items.text: None of the four boys has come.
None has done it.

blocks.text: **5. Demonstrative Pronoun**
This, that, these, those etc. को demonstrative pronoun कहते है।
**This:-** This का प्रयोग नजदीक के लिए होता है। This का प्रयोग हमेशा singular के रूप में करें।

items.text: This is a pen.
This is my book.

blocks.text: **That:-** That का प्रयोग दूर के लिए होता है। That का प्रयोग हमेशा singular के रूप में करें।

items.text: That is a book.
That is your pen.

blocks.text: **These:-** These का प्रयोग नजदीक के लिए करें। These का प्रयोग हमेशा plural के रूप में करें।

items.text: These are pens.
These are his books.

blocks.text: **Those:-** Those का प्रयोग दूर के लिए करें। those का प्रयोग हमेशा plural के रूप में करें।

items.text: Those are your books.
Those are trees.

blocks.text: **6. Reciprocal pronoun**

Each other और one another को reciprocal pronoun कहते हैं।
Each other का प्रयोग दो के लिए और one another का प्रयोग दो से अधिक के लिए करते हैं।

items.text: The two boys know each other.
The five students helped one another.

blocks.text: **7. Interrogative pronoun**
Interrogative pronouns का प्रयोग प्रश्न पूछने के लिए होता है जैसे who, whom, which, whose et.
**Who**
who का प्रयोग subject के लिए होता हैं।

items.text: who helped you?
who wrote the Ramayana?

blocks.text: **Whom**
Whom का प्रयोग object के लिए होता है

items.text: Whom do you blame?
Whom has Ravi helped?

blocks.text: **Whose**
Whose का प्रयोग owner (मालिक) के लिए होता है। whose का प्रयोग non - living के लिए ना करे।

items.text: Whose car is this?
Whose diamond is that?

blocks.text: **Which**
Which का प्रयोग दो या दो से अधिक व्यक्ति या वस्तु में से किसी एक को चुनने के लिए होता है।

items.text: Which of the two boys do you like most?
Which of these books do you like most?

blocks.text: **Some miscellaneous Rules**
**Rule -1**
Singular Noun को दोबारा लिखने के बजाय 'that of' का प्रयोग करें और plural noun को दोबारा लिखने के बजाय 'those of' का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: The climate of Bangalore is better than that of Delhi.
The rivers of America are larger than those of Europe.

blocks.text: **Rule-2**
Proposition के बाद whom का प्रयोग करें ना कि who का।

items.text: By who was the Ramayana written? (×)
By whom was the Ramayana written? (✓)

blocks.text: **Rule-3**
meaning को स्पष्ट करने के लिए Relatve pronoun का प्रयोग उस word के ठीक बाद करें जिसकी बात हो रही हैं।

items.text: I have read the plays of shakespeare which are very interesting. (×)
I have read shakespeare's plays which are very interesting. (✓)

blocks.text: **Rule-4**
Relative Pronoun के पहले possessive Adjective का प्रयोग ना करें।

items.text: Your car that you have needs repairing. (×)
The car that you have needs repairing. (✓)

blocks.text: **Rule-5**

इन words के बाद Relative pronoun के पहले 'those' का प्रयोग करें:-
Chiefly, mainly, namely, notably, including, especially, particularly et.

items.text: All the students, Mainly those who have come, are brilliant.
We need teachers especially those who are good at English.

blocks.text: **Rule -6**
यदि Sentence में Relative clause हो तो principle clause में personal pronoun का प्रयोग subject के रूप में ना करे।

items.text: The boy who has come late he is wicked. (×)
The boy who has come late is wicked. (✓)

blocks.text: 10. Article
Article is called a noun marker. It is a kind of adjective.
Article को संज्ञा को चिहित (Mark) करने वाला कहा गया है। यह एक प्रकार का Adjective होता है।
Article दो प्रकार के होते है:-

items: Indefinite Article (A/An)
Definite Article (The)

blocks.text: 1. Indefinite Article (A/An)
इनका प्रयोग Singular Noun के पहले होता है।

items.text: A __boy__ has come. (S.N.)
He is an __Orator__ (S.N)

blocks.text: Indefinite Article के कुछ Important Rules
Rule - 1

**इन Idioms या Phrases में हमेशा Article 'a' का प्रयोग करें**
a lot of, a number of, a large number of, eat a humble pie, in a hurry, in a rage, in a temper, at a loss, in a fix, in a dilemma, make a noise, have a drink, have a chat, take a rest, take a risk, take a break, run a temperature etc.

items.text: He was running a temperature.
She is in a temper.

blocks.text: Rule-2
Vowel sound words के साथ an का प्रयोग करें और consonant sound words के साथ 'a' का प्रयोग करें।
An
an apple, an M.A. an Editor, an umbrella, an excuse, an heir, an exppert, an FIR, an S.I. etc.
A
A cat, a bat, a mat, a member, a ewe, a euphemism, a year, a European lady, a university, a one way ticket, a B. A., a C. A. etc.
Rule-3
A/ An का प्रयोग पूरी जाती (Total class) के लिए हो सकता है।

items.text: A cow is a useful animal (सभी गायें)
A book is useful (सभी पुस्तकें)

blocks.text: Ruel-4
A/ An का प्रयोग exclamatory sentences में singular और uncountable noun के पहले करते हैं।

items.text: what a beauty!
what a handsome man!

blocks.text: Rule-5
किसी भी Profession (पेशा) के पहले article a/an का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: I am a teacher.
She is an engineer.

blocks.text: 2. Definite Article (The)
The का प्रयोग singular, plural और uncountable noun के पहले कर सकते हैं।
Some Important rules of 'The'
Rule-1
Holy book, river, sea, ocean etc. के नाम के पहले the का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: The Ganga is a pious river.
Do you know something about the pacific ocean?
Who wrote the Ramayana?

blocks.text: **Note:-** यदि holy book के पहले writer का name लिखा हो तो the का प्रयोग ना करें।

items.text: Valmiki 's Ramayana.

blocks.text: Rule - 2
Chain of mountains, group of islands, directions और newspaper के name के पहले the का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: The Himalayas are splendid.
He went to the East.

blocks.text: Rule-3
Adjective के Comparative degree के बाद यदि than/ to लिखा हो तो उसके पहले the ना लिखे।

items.text: He is the better than I. (✗)
He is better than I. (✓)
यदि Adjective के Comparative degree के बाद 'of the two' लिखा हो तो उसके पहले the लिखें।
He is the better of the two boys.
यदि regular increase (लगातार बढ़ने) या regular decrease (लगातार घटने) की बात हो तो दोनों Comparative degrees के पहले the लिखें।
The bigger, the better.
The more he gets, the more he wants.

blocks.text: Rule-4
इन words के पहले the का प्रयोग करें:-top, bottom, centre, middle, base, climate, weather, mountain, world, sun etc.

items.text: The weather is good.
He is at the bottom.

blocks.text: Rule-5
सामान्यत: noun + of + noun में पहले noun के साथ the का प्रयोग करते हैं।

items.text: The boys of this school are good.
He was appointed to the post of S. P.

blocks.text: Rule-6

यदि musical instruments का प्रयोग Primary purpose (मुख्य उद्देश्य) के लिए हो तो उनके पहले the का प्रयोग करे और यदि उनका प्रयोग Secondary purpose (किसी अन्य उद्देश्य) के लिए हो तो उनके पहले a/an का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: He is playing the guitar. **(Primary purpose)**
He has bought a guitar **(Secondary purpose)**

blocks.text: Rule-7
Single island, mountain's peak, season, lunch, breakfast, dinner etc. के पहले article का प्रयोग ना करें।

items.text: Java is an island.
Mount Everest is in Nepal.

blocks.text: Rule-8
Bay, gulf, train, political party, nationality, post etc. के नाम के पहले the का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: The Bay of Bengal.
The Rajdhani Express.
The BJP is in power.

blocks.text: Rule-9
जब common noun का प्रयोग abstract noun के रूप में हो तो उसके पहले the का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: The father in him woke up.
The Judge in him gave this decision.

blocks.text: **Note :-** जब common noun का प्रयोग abstract noun के रूप में हो तो preposition 'in' का प्रयोग जरूर होगा।
Rule - 10
Parts of body (शरीर के अंगों) के पहले the का प्रयोग करें यदि उनके पहले कोई preposition या बीमारी का नाम लिखा हो।

items.text: He hit me on the head.
Cancer of the lungs.

blocks.text: Rule - 11

**इन phrases में the का प्रयोग करें**
In the wrong, in the right, turn to the left, for the time being, on the whole, the whole, speak the truth , in the nick of time, by the way, what is the matter, what is the problem etc.

items.text: The whole Rome was burned.
You are in the wrong.

blocks.text: Rule-12
Scientific invention, government branches, और ordinal adjective के पहले the का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: Who invented the gun ?
The Judiciary is united.
The first president of India.

blocks.text: **Exception:** यदि Television के पहले on या watch लिखा तो किसी article का प्रयोग ना करें।

items.text: I am watching TV.

blocks.text: Rule - 13
**Each of / Either of / Neither of / Most of / None of** + the + Noun

items.text: Most of the boys have come.
Either of the brothers is there.

blocks.text: Rule-14
यदि किसी व्यक्ति ने नाम के पहले Adjective लिखा हो तो उसके पहले the लिखें।

items.text: The great Rakesh.
The young Rahul.

blocks.text: Rule-15
यदि किसी country का नाम plural हो तो उसके पहले the का प्रयोग करें जैसे The west Indies, The philippines, The Netherlands,The united states of America etc.
Omission of Articles or Zero Article
Rule-1
किसी भी subject, language, game और sport के नाम के पहले किसी article का प्रयोग ना करें।

items.text: Cricket is a nice game.
He teaches History.

blocks.text: **Exception :** Billiards के पहले हमेशा article the का प्रयोग करें।
Rule-2

**इन phrases के बाद किसी article का प्रयोग ना करें**
kind of, type of, sort of, variety of , post of, rank of, title of, a lot of, lots of, a number of, a good many, a great many, a large, number of etc.

items.text: I don't like that kind of boy.
A lot of boys were playing.

blocks.text: **Exception :** लेकिन interrogative sentence में kind of, type of और sort of के बाद a/ an का प्रयोग कर सकते हैं।

items.text: What kind of a man are you?

blocks.text: Rule - 3
यदि इन words का प्रयोग broad way (वृहद रूप) में हो या ये total class को दर्शाये तो इनके साथ किसी article का प्रयोग ना करें:-
man, woman, nature, death, music, science, life, freedom, humanity, mankind etc.

items.text: Man is a social animal
Nature is wonderful.

blocks.text: Rule -4
यदि इन words का प्रयोग primary purpose (मुख्य उद्देश्य) के लिए हो तो इनके साथ किसी article का प्रयोग ना करे:-
school, college, office, church, temple, jail, prison, hospital, bed, market etc.

items.text: I went to church to offer my prayer.
She was admitted to hospital.

blocks.text: **Note :-** Primary purpose का अर्थ है वे सभी चीजें जो जिस चीज के लिए बनी है जैसे school पढ़ने और पढ़ाने के लिए temple पूजा करने के लिए बना है इत्यादि
Rule-5

Possessive Adjective,/**(my, her, your etc.)** demonstrative / **(this, that etc.)** adjective और distributive adjective/**(each, either, etc.)** के बाद जिस Noun का प्रयोग हो उसके पहले किसी article का प्रयोग ना करें।

items.text: This is the my house. (✗)
This is my house. (✓)

blocks.text: Rule-6

**इन phrases में किसी article का प्रयोग ना करें**
by sea, by water, by air, by cheque, by chance, by mistake, by messenger, by trade, by profession, by post, by money order, in fact, in short, in detail, in confusion, in demand, in doubt, in debt etc.

items.text: I am in doubt.
I am in confusion.

blocks.text: 11. Infinitives
It is a kind of verb which has some features of noun also. यह एक प्रकार का verb है जिसमें noun के भी गुण होते हैं।
Infinitive दो प्रकार के होते हैं:-

items: Normal Infinitive
Bare Infinitive

blocks.text: 1. **Normal Infinitive** की पहचान to + v₁ होती है जैसे to go, to sleep, to eat, to speak, to work et.
2. **Bare infinitive** कि पहचान v₁ होती है जैसे go, sleep, eat, speak, work, hide etc.
Some important rules of Infinitive
Rule-1
किसी भी Modal के बाद Bare Infinitive का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: She can do it.
We must speak.

blocks.text: Rule-2
**Sub + verb + noun / pronoun + Bare Infinitive**
इस formula में प्रयोग होने वाले verbs ये है :-
See, watch, hear, notice, taste, observe, let, help, make, bid etc.
**Note:-** इस formula का प्रयोग सिर्फ active voice में करें।

items.text: I saw Ram watch Television.
They made me do the work.

blocks.text: Rule-3
**sub + H.v + v₃ + Normal Infinitive**
इस formula में प्रयोग होने वाले v₃ ये है:-
Seen, watched, heard, noticed, tasted, observed, helped, made, bidden etc.
**Note:-** इस formula का प्रयोग सिर्फ passive voice में करें।

items.text: She was seen to watch Television.
I was made to do the work.

blocks.text: Rule-4
सामान्यत: इन words के बाद Bare Infinitive का प्रयोग करते हैं:-
except, but, than , or etc.

items.text: He did not do anything but curse her.
I have nothing to eat except drink water.

blocks.text: Rule-5
सामान्यत: split infinitive (to + - + v₁) का प्रयोग नहीं करते।

items.text: He ordered me to beautifully decorate the house. (✗)
He ordered me to decorate the house beautifully.(✓)

blocks.text: Rule-6
जब दो Normal infinitives को किसी conjunction से जोड़ा जाए तो दूसरे normal infinitive के पहले 'to' ना लिखें।

items.text: To see and to believe are two different things. (✗)
To see and believe are two different things. (✓)

blocks.text: Rule-7
सामान्यत: इन verbs के बाद normal infinitive का प्रयोग करते हैं:-
agree, arrange, care, cease, attempt, consent, determine, decide, fall, hesitate, hope, learn, manage, neglect, prepare, promise, seem, swear, regret etc.

items.text: I hope to win the match.
They agreed to help us.

blocks.text: Rule-8
सामान्यत: इन verbs के बाद और normal infinitive के पहले 'how' का प्रयोग करते हैं:-
know, show, teach, wonder, ask, enquire, decide, learn etc.

items.text: He knows swimming. (✗)
He knows how to swim.(✓)
Please teach me to teach. (✗)
Please teach me how to teach.(✓)

blocks.text: Rule-9
why और why not के साथ Bare infinitive का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: why not go there?
why speak the truth?

blocks.text: Rule-10
सामान्यत: इन Adjectives के बाद **normal infinitive** का प्रयोग करते है:-
Unable, able, anxious, glad, happy, shocked, pleased, surprised, lucky, likely, right, wrong, delight, stupid, horrified etc.

items.text: I am shocked to hear it.
We were able to convince him.

blocks.text: Rule - 11
Dare not और Need not के बाद Bare infinitive का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: He dare not play chess.
You need not go there.

blocks.text: Rule - 12
इन phrases के बाद Bare infinitive का प्रयोग करें:-
had better, had rather, no sooner, would better, would rather etc.

items.text: You had better sleep now.
They would rather play than study.

blocks.text: 12. Gerund
When verb comes in 'ing' form without any helping verb and which acts as noun, it is called Gerund.
जब verb का प्रयोग 'ing' रूप में हो बिना किसी helping verb के और जिसका प्रयोग noun के रूप में हो उसे Gerund कहते हैं।

items.text: I like dancing
Swimming is good for health.

blocks.text: Some Important rules of Gerund
Rule -1
**Sub + verb + Gerund**

इस **formula** में प्रयुक्त होने वाले **verbs** हैं:- love, like, stop, start, hate, detest, miss, mind, keep, suggest, propose, continue, avoid, dislike, deny, can't help, can't imagine, enjoy, prefer, worth, adore, admit, bother, consider, endure, feel like, finish, imagine, keep on, recommend, resume etc.

items.text: He stopped talking to me.
Would you mind helping me?
We miss going there.

blocks.text: Rule-2

इन **prepositional phrases** के बाद Gerund का प्रयोग करें:-
be used to, get used to, habituated to, accustomed to, prone to, owing to, given to, taken to, committed to, devoted to, addicted to, averse to, in addition to, with a view to, objected to, look forward to etc.

items.text: I am used to teaching.
He is prone to making mistakes.
I am looking forward to meeting you.

blocks.text: Rule-3
इन **phrases** के बाद **Gerund** का प्रयोग करें:- no use, good use, much use, any use, some use, no point etc.

items.text: There is no use asking this.
There was much use knowing the truth.

blocks.text: Rule-4
Gerund के पहले Possessive Adjective या 's (apostrophe s) का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: He objected to Ram's going.
I like __his__ Singing. (P.A)

blocks.text: **Exception** लेकिन Passive voice में Gerund को पहले Possessive Adjective या 'S (apostrophe s) का प्रयोग ना करें।

items.text: I was shocked at Nitu being punished.
I am happy at Virat being awarded.

blocks.text: Rule-5
इन verbs के साथ Gerund और normal infinitive दोनों का प्रयोग कर सकते हैं:- begin, can't abide, can't bear, cease, commence, continue, intend etc.

items.text: It began to rain.
It began raining.

blocks.text: **Exception** लेकिन यदि sentence continuous tense में हो तो उन verbs के बाद normal infinitive (to + v₁) का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: I am beginning feeling better now. (✗)
I am beginning to feel better now. (✓)

blocks.text: Rule-6
**(a)** Allow + obj + Normal infinitive (to + v₁)
**(b)** Allow + Gerund (v₁ + ing)

items.text: He allowed singing.
He allowed me to sing.

blocks.text: Rule-7
Remember या Forget के साथ यदि Gerund का प्रयोग हो तो memory (यादों) की बात होगी और Remember या Forget के साथ यदि Normal infinitive का प्रयोग हो तो किसी कार्य को करने या ना करने की बात होगी।

items.text: Do you remember meeting him last year? **(Memory)**
I remember to post the letter. **(To Do Something)**

blocks.text: Rule-8
किसी भी preposition के बाद Gerund का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: She kept on Complaining.
They insisted upon going.
He abstained from drinking.

blocks.text: Rule-9

**इन phrases के बाद Gerund का प्रयोग करें**
have some problems, have no problem, have a hard time, have a good time, have an easy time, have fun etc.

items.text: I have fun dancing in the party.
she has difficulty reading a hindi newspaper.

blocks.text: Rule -10
Go + Gerund का प्रयोग Free time activities को करने के लिए होता है जैसे shopping, fishing, camping etc.

items.text: she went shopping.
we go fishing every sunday.

blocks.text: 13. Participle
It is a kind of Adjective. It is also called non-performing verb यह एक प्रकार का Adjective है।
इसे non-performing verb भी कहते हैं।
Participle तीन प्रकार के होते है:-

items: Present participle
Past Participle
Perfect Participle

blocks.text: 1. Present Participle
इसकी पहचान V₁+ing होती है।
Some important rules of present participle:
Rule-1
**Sub + verb + noun/pronoun + present participle**
इस **Formula** में प्रयोग होने वाले **verbs** है:-see, watch, hear, smell, feel, find, touch, notice, observe, taste etc.

items.text: I saw her watching television.
We heard them talking.

blocks.text: Rule-2
**Spend / Waste / Lose** + time/money + Present Participle

items.text: I spent two hours waiting for her.
We wasted ten lakh rupees satisfying them.

blocks.text: Rule-3
जब दो actions का वर्णण हो और दूसरा action पहले action का परिणाम (Result) हो तो दूसरे actioon में Present Participle का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: He shot, killing two birds.
Ravi fell, breaking his nose.

blocks.text: Rule-4
सामान्यत: Very के बाद Present Participle का प्रयोग करते हैं।

items.text: The movie is very interesting.
The news was very shocking.

blocks.text: Rule-5
While और when के बाद present participle का प्रयोग कर सकते हैं।

items.text: While playing cricket, he fell down.
He fell down when going down stairs.

blocks.text: 2. Past Participle
V₃ को past participle कहते हैं।
Important rules of past participle:
Rule-1
Past Participle का प्रयोग Adjective के रूप में होता है।

**Formula**
V₃ + Noun/Pronoun
or,
Noun/Pronoun + V₃

items.text: A broken chair
A deserted place
Spoken English

blocks.text: Rule-2
आदत (habit) या स्थिति (state) बताने के लिए।

items.text: A one eyed man.
Ill gotten wealth.

blocks.text: 3. Perfect Participle
Having + V₃ और Having + been + V₃ को perfect participle के नाम से जाना जाता है।
Having + V₃ का प्रयोग active voice में करते हैं और having + been + V₃ का प्रयोग Passive voice में करते हैं।

items.text: Having taken tea, he went to office.
Having been disturbed by the children, the man left the park.
Having risen the sun, the man woke up.

blocks.text: Adjective

items: An adjective is a word which qualifies noun or pronoun.
Adjective वह शब्द है जो Noun या Pronoun की विशेषता बताता है।

blocks.text: **Adjective** को दो प्रकार से **use** कर सकते हैं:-

items: Attributive use
Predicative use.

blocks.text: 1. **Attributive use:-** जब Adjective को noun या pronoun के पहले लिखते हैं तो उसे attributive use कहते हैं।

items.text: He is a __good__ boy.  (Attributive / Noun)
This is a __New__ house.  (Attributive / Noun)

blocks.text: **Predicative use:-** जब Adjective का प्रयोग Noun या Pronoun के बाद हो तो उसे Predicative use कहते हैं।

items.text: __Ram__ is __handsome__  (Noun / predicative)
__we__ are __intelligent__  (Pronoun / Predicative)

blocks.text: DEGREES OF ADJECTIVE
Adjective के तीन degrees होते है:-

items: Positive degree.
Comparative degree.
Superlative degree.

blocks.text: 1. **Positive degree:-** जब कोई तुलना ना कि जाए तब Positive degree का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: Ram is a __good__ boy.  (P.D.)

blocks.text: 2. **Comparative degree:** जब एक की तुलना दूसरे से हो तब comparative degree का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: Ram is __better__ than Ravi.  (C.D.)

blocks.text: 3. **Superlative Degree.** जब एक की तुलना सभी से हो तब Superlative degree का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: Ram is the __best__ boy in the school.  (S.D.)

blocks.text: Some important rules of Adjective

**Rule-1**
As या so के बाद हमेशा Adjective के positive degree का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: Ram is as good as I.
She is not so beautiful as I.

blocks.text:
**Rule-2**
'Preferable' का प्रयोग हमेशा comparative degree में करें। Preferable के साथ preposition 'to' का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: Tea is preferable to coffce.

blocks.text: Preferable के पहले more या most का प्रयोग ना करें।

**Rule-3**
A. As + P.D. + As + conjunction + C.D. + Than
B. C.D. + Than + conjunction + As + P.D. + AS
उपरोक्त Formulas का प्रयोग तभी करें जब एक ही sentence में positive degree और comparative degree का प्रयोग करना हो।

items.text: Ravi is as fast as and more laborious than his brother.
Ravi is more laborious than and as fast as his brother.

blocks.text:
**Rule-4**
**More + P.D + Than + P.D.**
जब एक ही व्यक्ति या वस्तु के गुणों के बीच तुलना हो तो इस formula का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: Ram is more good than laborious.
Your cow is more humble than wild.

blocks.text:
**Rule-5**
इन Adjective का प्रयोग **comparative** और **Superlative** degree में ना करें: Major, minor, unique, round, supreme, impossible, empty, infinite, absolute, complete, full, last, first, excellent etc.

items.text: Your mind is empty.
The major problem is poverty.

blocks.text:
**Rule-6**
जब सिर्फ दो व्यक्तियों या बस्तुओं के बीच तुलना हो तो superlative degree का प्रयोग ना करें।

items.text: He is the better of the two brothers.
Of the two students, he is the wiser.

blocks.text:
**Rule-7**
Verbs of sensation (ज्ञानेन्द्रियों से सम्बन्धित क्रियाएँ) जैसे कि look, feel, smell, taste, hear, see etc. की विशेषता Adjective बताता है।

items.text: The soup smells delicious.
She felt bad.

blocks.text:
**Rule-8**
**Some/Any**
**Some:-** Some का प्रयोग positive sentence में plural और uncountable noun के पहले करें।

items.text: I have some money.
They had some friends

blocks.text: **Any:-** Any का प्रयोग negative और Interrogative sentence में singular, plural और uncountable, noun के पहले करें।

items.text: I do not have any water.
Do you have any money?
He did not put any pens in the box.

blocks.text:
**Rule-9**
**Much/Many**
**Much:-** Much का प्रयोग हमेशा uncountable noun के पहले करें।

items.text: They had much information.
I have got much money.

blocks.text: **Many:-** Many का प्रयोग हमेशा Plural noun के पहले करें।

items.text: He has many books.
They have got many students.

blocks.text:
**Rule-10**
**Fewer/Less**
**Fewer:-** Fewer का प्रयोग हमेशा plural noun के पहले करें।

items.text: No fewer than twenty boys have come.
He knows no fewer than five languages.

blocks.text: **Less:-** Less का प्रयोग हमेशा uncountable noun के पहले करें।

items.text: You have less knowledge than I.
She has less money than we.

blocks.text:
**Rule-11**
'ior' से खत्म होने वाले Adjectives के साथ Preposition 'to' का प्रयोग करें जैसे कि:- Senior, jounior, superior, inferior, interior, exterior, prior etc.

items.text: She is senior to me.
Ram is junior to Ravi.

blocks.text:
**Rule-12**
**Last/Latest**
**Last:-** Last का प्रयोग order (क्रम) को बताने के लिए होता है।

items.text: He is the last man.
This is your last chance

blocks.text: **Latest:** Latest का प्रयोग time या new के लिए होता है।

items.text: This is the latest technology.
This is the latest news.

blocks.text:
**Rule-13**
**Later/Latter**
**Later:** Later का प्रयोग time (बाद में) बताने के लिए होता है।

items.text: He came later.
I will meet you later.

blocks.text: **Latter:** Latter का प्रयोग order (बाद वाला) बताने के लिए होता है।

items.text: Ram and Ravi are good students but I like the latter one.
The president and the P.M. are coming but I prefer the latter one.

blocks.text:
**Rule-14**
Lesser और worser को छोड़कर double comparative का प्रयोग ना करें। Lesser और worser के बाद 'than' का प्रयोग ना करें।

items.text: He is more stronger than I. ×
He is stronger than I. ✓
He is a lesser poet. ✓

blocks.text:
**Rule-15**
**Further/Farther**
**Further:** Further का प्रयोग extra (और) के लिए होता है।

items.text: Further I want to say this
I think further enquiry is needed.

blocks.text: **Farther:** Farther का प्रयोग distance (दूरी) के लिए होता है।

items.text: Don't go farther.
He went farther.

blocks.text:
**Rule-16**
**Subject + verb + noun + adjective + preposition + noun**

items.text: He is a student dear to all the teachers.
He is a man honest in his dealings.

blocks.text:
**Rule-17**
**P.D. + Conjunction + P.D.**
**C.D. + conjunction + C.D.**
**S.D. + conjunction + S.D.**
Conjunction के दोनों तरफ Adjective के same degrees का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: Ram is good and wise.
Ram is better and wiser than Ravi.
Amitabh is the tallest and most handsome actor in India.

blocks.text:
**Rule-18**
जब quality बढ़ने या घटने की बात हो तो दो compartive degree को conjunction 'and' से जोड़े।

items.text: The meeting became noiser and noiser.
He has grown weaker and weaker.

blocks.text:
**Rule-19**
**More + Than + P.D.**

items.text: She is more than kind to me.
We are more than smart.

blocks.text:
**Rule-20**
इन Phrases के साथ Comparative और Superlative degree दोनों का प्रयोग कर सकते हैं:- Very often, a lot, much, by far, a good deal etc.

items.text: He is a lot better now.

blocks.text:
**Rule-21**
**Nearest/Next**
**Nearest:** Nearest का प्रयोग Place (सबसे नजदीक जगह) के लिए करें।

items.text: My house is nearest from that station.
Which is the nearest restaurant?

blocks.text: **Next:** Next का प्रयोग order (क्रम) के लिए होता है।

items.text: He appealed to the next court.
My house is next to the station.

blocks.text:
**Rule-22**
**A/An + C.D. + Singular Noun + Than**

items.text: He is a better batsman than I.
This is an older tree than that one.

blocks.text:
**Rule-23**
जब sentence में एक से अधिक Adjective का प्रयोग हो तो उन्हें इस क्रम (order) में लिखें:-
OSSACENN
Opinion, size, shape, Age, colour, Emotion, Nationality और Noun.

items.text: This is a __big__(size) __sharp__(shape) __knife__(noun).
He is a __twenty year old__(age) __black__(colour) __American__(Nationality) __Man__(Noun).

blocks.text:
**Rule-24**
**Elder/Older**
**Elder:** जब तुलना (comparison) नहीं करना हो तो elder का प्रयोग करें। Elder के साथ Preposition 'to' का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: My elder brother lives here.
Mr. Sharma's elder son.

blocks.text: **Older:** Older का प्रयोग तुलना (comparison) के लिए करें। older के लिए 'than' का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: Ram is older than I.
My brother is older than I by Five minutes.

blocks.text:
**Rule-25**
All या both के बाद possessive adjective का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: __My__ all books. ×  (P.A.)
All __My__ books. ✓  (P.A.)

blocks.text: Adverb

items: An adverb is a word that qualifies verb, adjectvie or another adverb.
Adverb verb, adjective और अपने जैसे किसी अन्य adverb की विशेषता बताता हैं:-

blocks.caption: Adverb आठ प्रकार (8 types) के होते हैं
items: Adverb of manner: ये कार्य के होने के तरीके को बताते हैं जैसे Slowly, honestly, gladly, hard, fast etc.
Adverb of time: ये कार्य के होने के समय को बताते हैं जैसे now, today, the previous day, ago, recently etc.
Adverb of place: ये कार्य के होने के स्थान को बताते हैं जैसे here, there, everywhere, on a hill etc.
Adverb of degree: ये कार्य कितना हुआ यह बताता है जैसे quite, much, too, fairly, rather etc.
Adverb of number: ये कार्य कितनी बार हुआ यह बताता है जैसे once, twice, always, never, daily etc.
Adverb of reason: ये कार्य के होने के कारण को बताते हैं जैसे because, since, consequently, on account of, as etc.
Adverb of affirmation: हाँ कहने वाले अधिकतर words adverb होते हैं जैसे surely, truly, absolutely, why not etc.
Adverb of negation: ना कहने वाले अधिकतर words Adverb होते हैं जैसे not, never, hardly, rarely, scarcely etc.

blocks.text: Some Important Rules of Adverb

**Rule-1**
**Too:** Too का अर्थ आवश्यकता से अधिक होता है। इसका प्रयोग Negative sense (नकारात्मक भाव) में होता है और इसके बाद हमेशा positive degree का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: He is too bad.
You are too careless.

blocks.text:
**Rule-2**
**Too + Much + Noun**

items.text: His behaviour gives us too much Pain.
He has to bear too much carelessness.

blocks.text:
**Rule-3**
**Much + Too + Adjective**

items.text: You are much too bad.
They were much too careless.

blocks.text:
**Rule-4**
'Enough' का प्रयोग Adjective के Positive degree के ठीक बाद करें।

items.text: He is tall enough to touch the ceiling fan.
The policeman was brave enough to catch the thief.

blocks.text:
**Rule-5**
Quite का अर्थ totally, completely, all, absolutely etc. हो सकता है। quite के बाद सामान्यत: adjective के comparative degree या V3 का प्रयोग करते हैं।

items.text: He is quite taller.
They were quite tired.

blocks.text: Quite के साथ all, totally, completely, absolutely etc. words का प्रयोग ना करें।

**Rule-6**
इन verbs के बाद 'as' का प्रयोग ना करें:- call, name, elect, select, appoint, think, make, choose, consider etc.

items.text: He was elected chairman.
He called me a liar.

blocks.text:
**Rule-7**
इन words के बाद not या never का प्रयोग ना करें:- deny, prevent, prohibit, forbid, hardly, rarely, barely, scarcely, seldom, little, few, both, too-to, lest etc.

items.text: He prevented me from going.
He barely goes there.

blocks.text:
**Rule-8**
**Fairly:** Fairly का प्रयोग हमेशा अच्छे Adjectives के positive degree के साथ करें। Fairly के साथ unpleasant adjective का प्रयोग ना करें।

items.text: He is fairly handsome.
She is fairly tall.

blocks.text:
**Rule-9**
**Rather**
Rather का प्रयोग unpleasant adjectives (बुरे adjectives) के साथ करें। Rather के साथ Positive और Comparative दोनों degrees का प्रयोग कर सकते हैं।

items.text: She is rather bad.
I am rather worse.

blocks.text:
**Rule-10**
A. The + very + superlative degree.
B. Much + The + superlative degree.

items.text: I am the very best.
She is much the best.

blocks.text:
**Rule-11**
A. Very + Poistive degree.
B. Much + comparative Degree.

items.text: He is very good.
You are much worse.

blocks.text: Much का प्रयोग हमेशा bad qualities के लिए होता है।

**Rule-12**
**At present/Presently**
**At present:** At present का प्रयोग present countinuous tense में करते हैं।

items.text: I am working at present.

blocks.text: **Presently:** Presently का प्रयोग future tense में करते हैं।

items.text: Train will come presently.
The show will start presently.

blocks.text:
**Rule-13**
**Always/Ever**
**Always:** Always का प्रयोग हमेशा के अर्थ में होता है। Always का प्रयोग subject तथा main verb के बीच में होता है।

items.text: He always helps me.
I always go there.

blocks.text: **Ever:** Ever का प्रयोग कभी भी के अर्थ में होता है। इसका प्रयोग interrogative और negative sentence में होता है।

items.text: Have you ever been to Delhi?
We hardly ever go there.

blocks.text: POSITION OF ADVERBS
Exam में adverb के positions से बहुत questions पुछे जाते हैं। Position of Adverbs के कुछ important rules:

**Rule-1**
सामान्यत: MPT formula का प्रयोग किया जाता है।
M → Adverb of Manner
P → Adverb of Place
T → Adverb of Time

items.text: He worked __honestly__(Manner) __in the garden__(Place) __yesterday__(Time)
They lived __happily__(Manner) __in this house__(Place) __for a year__(Time).

blocks.text:
**Rule-2**
सामान्यत: Adverb of Manner का प्रयोग sentence के अंत में करते हैं। लेकिन passive voice में adverb of manner का प्रयोग main verb के ठीक पहले या बाद में कर सकते हैं।

items.text: I did the work carefully
The work was done __Carefully__(Manner).
The work was __Carefully__(Manner) done.

blocks.text:
**Rule-3**
'How' से शुरू होने वाले sentence में Adverb of manner का प्रयोग 'how' के ठीक बाद करें।

items.text: How beautifully he plays!
How well they do it!

blocks.text:
**Rule-4**
सामान्यत: Adverb of number का प्रयोग verb के पहले करते हैं।

items.text: We __often__(number) __go__(verb) there.
He __usually__(number) __goes__(verb) to school by bus.

blocks.text:
**Rule-5**
सामान्यत: Adverb of number का प्रयोग used to, has to, have to, had to etc. के पहले करते हैं।

items.text: He always used to help me.
I often have to go there.

blocks.text:
**Rule-6**
सामान्यत: Adverb of degree का प्रयोग verb के पहले करते हैं।

items.text: She __nearly__(degree) __fell__(verb).
I __quite__(Degree) __agree__(verb) with you.

blocks.text:
**Rule-7**
सामान्यत: PFT formula का प्रयोग किया जाता है।
P – Adverb of place
F – Adverb of Frequency
T – Adverb of Time

items.text: I have been __to Delhi__(Place) __Several times__(Frequency) __this year__(time).
He goes __to London__(Place) __every year__(Frequency) __now a days__(Time).

blocks.text: When verb is used before subject it is called inversion.
Inversion

An inversion can only be applied if a sentence starts with Adverb. Inversion is used to emphasize a sentence. जब क्रिया का प्रयोग subject के पहले हो तो उसे inversion कहते हैं। Inversion का प्रयोग तभी करें जब sentence की शुरुआत Adverb से हो। इसका प्रयोग sentence पर जोड़ डालने के लिए करते हैं।
Inversion दो प्रकार के होते हैं:-

items: Partial inversion
complete inversion

blocks.text:
**1. Partial inversion**
Partial inversion में helping verb subject के पहले रहता है और main verb subject के बाद रहता है।

**1. Partial inversion का formula:-**
**Adverb + H.V. + sub. + M.V. + .....**

items.text: Never do we play chess.
Hardly does she go there.

blocks.text:
**2. Complete inversion**
Complete inversion में helping verb और main verb दोनों का प्रयोग subject के पहले होता है।

complete inversion का formula:-
**Adverb of place + H.V. + M.V. + Sub**

items.text: On a hill am sitting I.
In the sky fly birds.

blocks.text: Inversion के कुछ important rules

**Rule-1**
यदि sentence की शुरुआत इनमें से किसी भी Adverbs से हो तो partial inversion का प्रयोग करें:- hardly, scarcely, rarely, barely, seldom, never, no sooner etc.

items.text: Seldom does Ram come her.
Rarely do they go there.

blocks.text:
**Rule-2**
यदि sentence की शुरुआत only + Adverb से हो तो partial inversion का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: Only last month did he do it.
Only by working hard did he get success.

blocks.text: Rule-3
यदि sentence की शुरूआत इन phrases से हो तो partial inversion का प्रयोग करें:

items: in no way, on no account, at no cost, on no condition, under no circumstances etc.

items.text: In no way will he help us.
At no cost did he tell me.

blocks.text: Rule-4
यदि sentence की शुरूआत इन Adverbs of place से हो तो complete inversion का प्रयोग करें:

items: on a hill, round the corner, just outside the gate, in the sky, along the northern frontier of India, under the table, on a chair etc.

items.text: On a chair is sitting Ram.
Under the table is lying a cat.

blocks.text: Rule-5
यदि sentence की शुरूआत so + Adverb of manner से हो तो partial inversion का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: So honestly did he work that everybody liked him.
So fast does he run that nobody can catch him.

blocks.text: Rule-6
यदि sentence की शुरूआत here, there, up, down, in, out, off etc. से हो और subject के स्थान पर noun हो तो complete inversion का प्रयोग करें।
**Here/There/Up/Down/in/out/off + verb + subject (noun).**

items.text: Here comes the chairman.
There was a king.

blocks.text: Rule-7
**As/So/Neither/Nor + HV + Subejct**

items.text: She is going to market and so am I.
She loves me and so do I.
I have not gone to America and neither has my sister.

blocks.text: 17. Preposition

Preposition is a word or group of words that is generally used before noun or pronoun to show their relation. Preposition शब्द या शब्दों का वह समूह है जिसका प्रयोग सामानयत: Noun या Pronoun के पहले करते है उनका सम्बन्ध बताने के लिए।
Basic concepts of some important prepositions
1. Use of Into
अन्दर की तरफ गति से (Inside with motion) के लिए into का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: He fell into the well.
He jumped into the river.

blocks.text: (ii) अवस्था परिवर्तन के लिए (From one state to other) into का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: Milk turns into curd.
Water turns into vapour.

blocks.text: (iii) सामान्यत: enter के साथ into का प्रयोग नहीं करते लेकिन यदि sentence में इनमें से किसी भी word का प्रयोग हो तो enter के साथ into का प्रयोग करें:-

items: friendship, partnership, alliance, agreement, contract, treaty, co-operation etc.

items.text: India and America entered into a contract.
Bokla and Agnes entered into friendship in 2007.

blocks.text: 2. Use of with
(i) with का प्रयोग साथ (together) के लिए किया जाता है।

items.text: I am with you.
Ram is sitting with Sita.

blocks.text: (ii) कोई भी यंत्र जब हमसे स्पर्श करें (instrument touching) तो with का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: He killed a tiger with a sword.
He writes with a Pen.

blocks.text: 3. Uses of At

Small place, small area, small town, age, rate, festival, temporary action, point of time, bottom, back, noon, midnight, sunrise, sunset, dawn, dusk, village name, street number etc. के साथ at का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: He will come at Holi.
I met him at sunset.
She came at 4 pm.

blocks.text: **Note-1.** Village name के साथ at का प्रयोग करते हैं लेकिन यदि सिर्फ village शब्द हो तो in का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: She lives at Bela (a village name)
She lives in a village.

blocks.text: **Note-2.** Back का अर्थ यदि पीछे हो तो at का प्रयोग करें लेकिन यदि back का अर्थ पीठ हो तो in का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: This book has an answer at the back.
He shot me in my back.

blocks.text: **Note-3.** Street number के साथ at का प्रयोग करते हैं लेकिन यदि number ना हो तो in का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: He lives in Dalal street.
He lives at 32, Dalal street.

blocks.text: 4. Uses of In
Big place, big area, big town, morning, afternoon, evening, night month, year, country, state, capital, district, famous place etc. के साथ in का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: He lives in India.
He came in the morning

blocks.text: (ii) यदि car, taxi, auto या helicopter के पहले किसी article का प्रयोग हो तो in का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: He went there in a car.
I came here in an auto.

blocks.text: (iii) यदि bus, train, jeep, boat, plane etc. के पहले किसी article का प्रयोग हो तो in और on दोनों का प्रयोग कर सकते हैं।

items.text: He goes to school in a jeep.
He went there on a bus.

blocks.text: (iv) यदि car, taxi, auto, helicopter, bus, train, jeep, plane etc. के पहले article ना हो तो by का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: I go there by car.
She came here by train.

blocks.text: 5. Uses of Across
(i) किसी चीज के उस पार (opposite side) के लिए across का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: She lives across the river.
My house is across the road.

blocks.text: (ii) Across का प्रयोग सभी जगह (everywhere) के लिए भी कर सकते हैं।

items.text: Malaria spread across the state.
There were terrorists across Pakistan.

blocks.text: (iii) इस पार से उस पार (From one side to the other) के लिए across का प्रयोग कर सकते हैं।

items.text: There is a bridge across the river.
He swam across the river.

blocks.text: 6. Uses of Through
के माध्यम से (by means of) के लिए through का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: I came to know it through a media report.
I have come here through a proper channel.

blocks.text: (ii) किसी माध्यम (medium) से होकर इस पार से उस पार (from one side to the other) के लिए through का प्रयोग करते हैं।

items.text: The train was passing through a tunnel.
They are passing through the door.

blocks.text: 7. Uses of Between
(i) दो व्यक्तियों या वस्तुओं के बीच में के लिए between का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: Ram is sitting between Sita and Gita.
Distribute it between Ram and Rani.

blocks.text: (ii) Between या अन्य किसी भी prepositions के बाद Pronoun के object form का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: There is friendship between him and her.
He will not come between you and me.

blocks.text: (iii) Between के साथ And का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: Nepal lies between India and China.

blocks.text: (iv) यदि इनमें से किसी भी word का प्रयोग sentence में हो तो between का प्रयोग दो से अधिक व्यक्तियों या वस्तुओं के लिए कर सकते हैं।

items: Friendship, partnership, alliance, treaty, agreement, contract, co-operation, tri-series etc.

items.text: A tri-series is played between India, England and Australia.
There is a contract between these twenty parties.

blocks.text: 8. Uses of Beside
Beside का प्रयोग के बगल में (by the side) के लिए किया जाता है।

items.text: She sat beside me.
They stood beside her.

blocks.text: 9. Use of Besides
Besides का प्रयोग के अलावा (in addition to) के लिए किया जाता है।

items.text: Besides chocolate, he gave me a pen.
Besides gun, he has a knife

blocks.text: Uses of Among, Amongst, Amid, Amidst
(i) Among, Amongst, Amid और Amidst का प्रयोग दो से अधिक व्यक्तियों या वस्तुओं के बीच में के लिए किया जाता है।

items.text: Among the boys, Ram is the best.

blocks.text: **Note:** उपरोक्त sentence में Among के स्थान पर amongst, amid या amidst भी लिख सकते हैं।
(ii) Vowel से शुरू होने वाले words के पहले सिर्फ amongst या amidst का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: **Amongs our sons, he is the best**

blocks.text: (iii) Uncountable noun के पहले सिर्फ Amid या amidst का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: He was searching his pen amid/amidst the luggage.

blocks.text: 11. Uses of Over
(i) कोई भी चीज जब सतह से स्पर्श ना करें (something not touching surface).

items.text: (i) The sun is over my head

blocks.text: (ii) कोई भी चीज जब पूरी तरह से cover हो (covered totally)

items.text: The umbrella is over my head

blocks.text: 12. Uses of under.
(i) कोई भी चीज जब सतह से स्पर्श ना करें अंदर से।

items.text: We are sitting under the tree.

blocks.text: 13. Uses of Above
(i) किसी चीज से उपर (higher than) के लिए above का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: The kite rose above the building.

blocks.text: (ii) संख्या या मात्रा में अधिक के लिए above का प्रयोग करते हैं।

items.text: His income is above Rs.50000/-

blocks.text: 14. Use of Below
(i) किसी चीज से नीचे के लिए below का प्रयोग करते हैं।

items.text: The water is below the danger level.

blocks.text: (ii) संख्या या मात्रा में कम के लिए below का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: They live below the poverty line.

blocks.text: 15. Uses of OFF
Off का प्रयोग अलगाव (separation) और दूरी (distance) के लिए होता है।

items.text: He fell off the lader.
Off goes the ball.

blocks.text: 16. Use of On
(i) कोई भी चीज जब सतह से स्पर्श करें तब on का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: The book is on the table.

blocks.text: (ii) किसी भी दिन (Day) और तारीख (Date) के पहले on का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: He will come on Monday.

blocks.text: 17. Use of By
(i) By का प्रयोग Near के लिए कर सकते है।

items.text: He sat by me.

blocks.text: (ii) एक निश्चित समय से पहले या एक निश्चित समय तक के लिए by का प्रयोग करें

items.text: He will come by 2pm.

blocks.text: (iii) यातायात के साधन के लिए by का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: He goes there by jeep.

blocks.text: Prepositions के कुछ Important rules
Rule:-1.
यदि **Infinitive (to + v₁)** का प्रयोग **Sentence** के अंत में हो तो उसके साथ के **Preposition** को लिख दें।

items.text: There is no house to live in.
Do you have a bed to sleep in?
I have nobody to eat with.

blocks.text: Rule:-2.
जब दो words को किसी conjunction से जोड़ा जाए तो उनके साथ के Prepositions को लिख दें।

items.text: Some animals can live both in water and on land.
He was ashamed of and shocked at his failure.

blocks.text: Rule:-3.
यदि इन Verbs के बाद किसी व्यक्ति का वर्णण हो तो Prepositions 'to' का प्रयोग करें:-

items: Say, suggest, propose, reply, explain, complain, talk, listen, write, pray, appeal etc.

items.text: They suggested to me.
Listen to me.

blocks.text: Rule-4.
सामान्यत: Adverb के पहले किसी Preposition का प्रयोग नहीं करते।

items.text: (i) He will come __tomorrow__ (adverb)
(ii) He lives __abroad__ (adverb)
(iii) Go __home__ (adverb)

blocks.text: **Note:-** यदि home के पहले go, reach, come, bring, get etc. लिखा हो तो home का प्रयोग adverb के रूप में होता है।
Rule:-5
यदि दिन या महीने के नाम के पहले इनमें से कोई भी शब्द लिखा हो preposition का प्रयोग न करें

items: next, last, each, every, this, that

items.text: He will go next Sunday.
They came last Monday.

blocks.text: Rule:-6
किसी भी preposition के बाद Gerund का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: (i) He kept on singing.
(ii) They entered the hall by buying the tickets.

blocks.text: Rule:-7
सामान्यत: इन Verb के साथ किसी preposition का प्रयोग नहीं करें:-

items: reach, resist, tell, order, attack, investigate, inform, obey, disobey, violate, despite, comprise, enter, discuss, describe, explain, request, ask, stress, emphasize, demand etc.

items.text: (i) He ordered the books.
(ii) We must discuss the matter.

blocks.text: **Note:-** comprise के साथ passive voice में preposition of का प्रयोग करते हैं।

items.text: our team is comprised of seven players.

blocks.text: Rule:-8
यात्रा के दौरान किसी place पर ठहरने (stay) के लिए preposition at का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: I stopped at Delhi on my way to London.

blocks.text: Rule:-9
यदि किसी कार्य को हम शरीर के अंगों (Body Parts) की सहायता से करें तो Preposition by का प्रयोग करें लेकिन यदि किसी कार्य को किसी चीज की सहायता से करे तो preposition with का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: (i) He killed a snake with a stick.
(ii) He opened the lid by his teeth.

blocks.text: 18. Modals & Verb
A doing word is called verb
वह शब्द जिससे कुछ होने का एहसास हो उसे verb कहते हैं।
**Verb** दो प्रकार के होते हैं:-

items: Helping Verb
Main Verb

blocks.text: 1. **Helping Verb:-** Helping Verbs तीन प्रकार के होते है:-

items: Modal
Primary Helping Verb
Partial Modal

blocks.text: 1. **Modal:** यह एक प्रकार का helping verb है जिसका एक से अधिक अर्थ हो सकता है। modal कभी भी main verb नहीं बन सकता है तथा इनके बाद हमेशा v₁ का प्रयोग करते हैं।
2. **Primary Helping Verb:-** Be, do और have को primary helping verb कहते हैं क्योंकि सभी helping verbs इन्हीं तीनों से बने है जैसे is, am, are, was, were, been, do, does, did, has, have, had, etc.
3. **Partial Modal:-** Dare, need और used to को partial modal कहते हैं। इनका प्रयोग modal के अलावा अन्य Parts of speech में भी हो सकता है इसलिए इन्हें partial modal कहते हैं।
Use of Modals
**Can**
**Rule-1** *Can* का प्रयोग power, capacity और ability के लिए करते हैं।

items.text: I can lift this bag.

blocks.text: **Rule-2** Can का प्रयोग friendly request के लिए कर सकते हैं।

items.text: Can I take your pen friend?

blocks.text: **Rule-3** Can का प्रयोग किसी व्यक्ति का nature या habit बताने के लिए कर सकते हैं।

items.text: An honest man can never cheat anyone

blocks.text: **Rule-4** Can का प्रयोग theoretical possibility (सैद्धांतिक सम्भावना) के लिए कर सकते हैं।

items.text: Electricity can be dangerous.

blocks.text: **Could**
**Rule-1** Can के past को could कहते हैं।

items.text: Ravi told me that he could help me.

blocks.text: **Rule-2** Can का प्रयोग past की ability, power या capacity के लिए कर सकते हैं।

items.text: He could drive a car at the age of sixteen.

blocks.text: **Rule-3** Office में polite request (विनम्र निवेदन) के लिए could का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: Could I talk to Mr. John?

blocks.text: **Rule-4** Could या can के साथ able या capable का प्रयोग ना करें।

items.text: He can be able to do it (✗)
He can do it. (✓)

blocks.text: **Rule-5** Rare possibility (बहुत ही कम सम्भावना) के लिए could का प्रयोग करते हैं।

items.text: There could be a bomb under your seat.

blocks.text: **May**
**Rule-1** May का प्रयोग permission लेने या देने के लिए करते हैं।

items.text: May I come in, sir?

blocks.text: **Rule-2** May का प्रयोग polite request (विनम्र निवेदन) के लिए करते हैं।

items.text: May I sit here?

blocks.text: **Rule-3** May का प्रयोग less possibility (कम सम्भावना) के लिए करते हैं।

items.text: It may rain today.

blocks.text: **Rule-4** यदि principle clause (मुख्य clause) present tense में हो और sub-ordinate clause (उप clause) की शुरूआत so that या in order that से हो तो sub-ordinate clause में may का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: **We eat** (principle clause) **So that we may live** (sub-ordinate clause)

blocks.text: **Might**
**Rule-1** May के past को might कहते हैं।

items.text: She said that she might do that

blocks.text: **Rule-2** Polite request (विनम्र निवेदन) के लिए old English में might का प्रयोग कर सकते है।

items.text: Might I come in, now?

blocks.text: **Rule-3** Might का प्रयोग rare possibility (बहुत ही कम सम्भावना) के लिए करते हैं।

items.text: It might rain today.

blocks.text: **Rule-4** Might या may के साथ likely और possibly का प्रयोग न करें।

items.text: He may likely go there (✗)
He may go there. (✓)

blocks.text: **Would**
**Rule-1** will के past को would कहते हैं।

items.text: He told me that he would play.

blocks.text: **Rule-2** Would का प्रयोग past habit के लिए कर सकते है।

items.text: He would play cricket daily.

blocks.text: **Rule-3** Would का प्रयोग polite request के लिए कर सकते हैं।

items.text: Would you please help me?

blocks.text: **Rule-4** Choice या preference के लिए would का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: I would rather die than beg.

blocks.text: **Rule-5** Would + sub + mind + gerund +.............?

items.text: Would you mind going there?

blocks.text: **Should**
**Rule-1** Shall के past को should कहते हैं।

items.text: He said that he should play.

blocks.text: **Rule-2** should का प्रयोग suggestion के लिए करते है।

items.text: You should study now.

blocks.text: **Rule-3** Lest के साथ should का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: He ran fast lest he should miss that train.

blocks.text: **Rule-4** Should का अर्थ if भी हो सकता है।

items.text: Should you work hard, you can get success.

blocks.text: **Must**
**Rule-1** Modern English में ought to के स्थान पर must का प्रयोग करें

items.text: We must help her.
or
We ought to help her.

blocks.text: **Rule-2** Must का अर्थ compulsion (बाध्यता) हो सकता है।

items.text: You must do as you are told.

blocks.text: **Rule-3** Strong moral obligation (तीव्र नैतिक कर्तव्य) के लिए must का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: We must respect our flag.

blocks.text: **Rule-4** लिखित आदेश या निर्देश (written order or instruction) के लिए must का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: All the workers must be at their desk by 10 am.

blocks.text: **Used to**
**Rule-1** used to का प्रयोग past habit के लिए करते है।
**sub + used to + v₁ + obj**

items.text: He used to play chess daily.

blocks.text: **Rule-2**
sub + **Be**/**Get** + used to + gerund

items.text: He is used to teaching.
I got used to speaking.

blocks.text: **Rule-3** जब appearance (आकृति) में बदलाव की बात हो तो used to का प्रयोग करें।

items.text: She used to have long hair.

blocks.text: **Dare/Need**
**Rule-1** Positive sentence में dare (साहस) और need (जरूरत) का प्रयोग main verb के रूप में होता है।

items.text: He __dares__ to play. (MV)

blocks.text: **Rule-2** सामान्यत: negative और interrogative sentences में dare और need का प्रयोग modal के रूप में होता है।

items.text: He __need__ not play. (modal)

blocks.text: **Rule-3** जब dare और need का प्रयोग modal के रूप में हो तो उनके बाद 'to' का प्रयोग न करें।

items.text: She dare not go there.

One Word Substitution

4. A person who abandons religion — (a) Egotist (b) Priest (c) Atheist (d) Apostate — **Ans: d Apostate**
5. A person who hates and avoids other people — (a) Hermit (b) Pervert (c) Misanthrope (d) Masochist — **Ans: c Misanthrope**
6. A person who does not believe that God or Gods exist. — (a) Blasphemous (b) Atheist (c) Irreverent (d) Profane — **Ans: b Atheist**
7. A book or set of books giving information about all areas of knowledge — (a) Dictionary (b) Encyclopedia (c) Anthology (d) Volume — **Ans: b Encyclopedia**
8. Huge fire for celebration — (a) Bonfire (b) Festivity (c) Firepower (d) Firefight — **Ans: a Bonfire**

91. mutate — (a) replicate (b) imitate (c) change (d) Incise — **Ans: c change**
92. satisfaction — (a) refreshment (b) pride (c) contentment (d) bliss — **Ans: c contentment**
93. Magnify — (a) Expand (b) Magnanimous (c) Miniature (d) Underemphasise — **Ans: a Expand**
94. Remorse — (a) Repentance (b) Slow (c) Liberate (d) Affinity — **Ans: a Repentance**
95. stagnant — (a) Motionless (b) Fatal (c) Unstable (d) Accident — **Ans: a Motionless**
96. void — (a) traditions (b) gap (c) memory (d) Experience — **Ans: b gap**
97. perils — (a) Delights (b) Benefits (c) Hazards (d) Humiliations — **Ans: c Hazards**
98. hysterical — (a) Emotional (b) Dangerous (c) Funny (d) Scary — **Ans: c Funny**
99. inundated — (a) Annoyed (b) Preoccupied (c) Rewarded (d) Overwhelmed — **Ans: d Overwhelmed**
100. Perseverance — (a) Discipline (b) Tolerance (c) Steadfastness (d) Humbleness — **Ans: c Steadfastness**

blocks.text: Antonyms

items: 1. Her dog can climb **under** the fence. — (a) Over (b) Sink (c) Behind (d) Beneath — **Ans: (a) Over**
2. Henry is so **servile** that other people take advantage of him. — (a) Arrogant (b) Sheepish (c) Bickering (d) Cunning — **Ans: (a) Arrogant**
3. He was **compelled** by everyone to join the group. — (a) Demur (b) Obscured (c) Confessed (d) Induced — **Ans: (d) Induced**
4. Do you know the old **adage** 'the show must go on'? — (a) Misconstruction (b) Rebuke (c) Dare (d) Addiction — **Ans: (a) Misconstruction**
5. Mr. Khanna is quite ______ **(callous)** towards the victims of earthquake. — (a) rationalistic (b) unsympathetic (c) sympathetic (d) Non-diplomatic — **Ans: (c) sympathetic**
6. 'Optimistic' — After the incident, Radha prepared herself to stay positive even in gloomy days. — (a) stay (b) incident (c) positive (d) Gloomy — **Ans: (d) Gloomy**
7. Compulsory — (a) Obligatory (b) Mandatory (c) Voluntary (d) Subsidiary — **Ans: (c) Voluntary**
8. He was riding his bike on a **bumpy** surface. — (a) Jerky (b) Uneven (c) Choppy (d) Steady — **Ans: (d) Steady**
9. Grow — (a) Shrink (b) Crimp (c) Plant (d) Alter — **Ans: (a) Shrink**
10. Instead of becoming **joyous** to hear the breaking news, she became ______ . — (a) melancholic (b) vengeful (c) ecstatic (d) Exuberant — **Ans: (a) melancholic**
11. His knowledge is **superficial** in nature. — (a) Deviant (b) Profound (c) Artificial (d) Opposing — **Ans: (b) Profound**
12. Absurd — (a) Sensible (b) Tolerable (c) Adorable (d) Lovable — **Ans: (a) Sensible**
13. Her parents **forbade** her to marry her lover. — (a) prevented (b) refused (c) declined (d) Allowed — **Ans: (d) Allowed**
14. We lived a very **frugal** life during our childhood. — (a) Reckless (b) Weary (c) Severe (d) Extravagant — **Ans: (d) Extravagant**
15. While most friends **persuaded** him to choose the career, there were a few who ______ him from doing so. — (a) evaded (b) bombarded (c) dissuaded (d) Situated — **Ans: (c) dissuaded**
16. One may **accomplish** many things by a little effort. — (a) Qualify (b) Retain (c) Execute (d) Miss — **Ans: (d) Miss**
17. The professor's lectures are very conventional and so **tedious**. — (a) Interesting (b) Informative (c) Idle (d) Boring — **Ans: (a) Interesting**
18. The musician gave a **dismal** performance of a few old melodies at last night's concert. — (a) smart (b) melancholy (c) exhausting (d) Lively — **Ans: (d) Lively**
19. She **chided** her son for being impatient with the customers. — (a) reproved (b) scolded (c) applauded (d) Rebuked — **Ans: (c) applauded**

20. 'disenfranchise' — The parliament of Tonoco authorised the king to take over the reins of the government and disable the power of the council of ministers. — (a) Authorised (b) Powers (c) Disabled (d) Reins — **Ans: (a) Authorised**
21. They **praised** her for her bravery. — (a) Acclaimed (b) Rebuked (c) Defended (d) Assaulted — **Ans: (b) Rebuked**
22. Abandon — (a) Quit (b) Surrender (c) Shorten (d) Continue — **Ans: (d) Continue**
23. I **confronted** the journalist with the evidence. — (a) Resisted (b) Avoided (c) Challenged (d) Encountered — **Ans: (b) Avoided**
24. Prohibition — (a) Restriction (b) Inhibition (c) Direction (d) Permission — **Ans: (d) Permission**
25. Selective — (a) Stimulating (b) Careless (c) Doubtful (d) Planned — **Ans: (b) Careless**
26. The crowd gave an **exultant** cheer to the football team when they were exiting the airport. — (a) rejoicing (b) disconsolate (c) jubilant (d) Elated — **Ans: (b) disconsolate**
27. The village fair was cancelled due to **incessant** rains. — (a) Insistent (b) Daily (c) Sporadic (d) Continual — **Ans: (c) Sporadic**
28. Trivial — (a) Significant (b) Huge (c) Heavy (d) Light — **Ans: (a) Significant**
29. I **object** the false claims made against me. — (a) assent (b) ease (c) restore (d) Ascertain — **Ans: (a) assent**
30. Conceal — (a) Watch (b) See (c) Reveal (d) Hide — **Ans: (c) Reveal**
31. Memory — (a) Suppress (b) Oblivion (c) Retrospection (d) Impart — **Ans: (b) Oblivion**
32. I don't like to work with people who are not **flexible**. — (a) Capable (b) Malleable (c) Open-ended (d) Rigid — **Ans: (d) Rigid**
33. My niece is an **amateur** artist. I hope she becomes famous one day. — (a) Boring (b) Freelancing (c) Expert (d) Decent — **Ans: (c) Expert**

34. 'Naïve' — After years of working in politics, she had become cynical and jaded, convinced that all politicians were corrupt and that the system was rigged against the people. — (a) Jaded (b) Corrupt (c) Cynical (d) Convinced — **Ans: (c) Cynical**
35. Israel has been a **staunch** ally of the United States. — (a) Careless (b) Confused (c) Unsteady (d) Resolute — **Ans: (c) Unsteady**
36. The thief was caught with a **spurious** Picasso painting. — (a) Authentic (b) Fake (c) Splendid (d) Stolen — **Ans: (a) Authentic**
37. The gradual ______ **(curtail)** in the expenditures made them tensed. — (a) innate (b) indent (c) insinuate (d) Increase — **Ans: (d) Increase**
38. Derision — (a) Disintegration (b) Admiration (c) Estimation (d) Termination — **Ans: (b) Admiration**
39. The Emperor was considered as a **wicked** man. — (a) Virtuous (b) Savage (c) Fierce (d) Vile — **Ans: (a) Virtuous**
40. Gullible — (a) Pliant (b) Cynical (c) Credulous (d) Simple — **Ans: (b) Cynical**
41. Is there any portal to **redeem** these coupons that I have collected from the store? — (a) forsake (b) forfeit (c) deplore (d) embezzle — **Ans: (b) forfeit**
42. Lucy presented a **lucid** account of her achievements before the committee. — (a) intelligible (b) transparent (c) ambiguous (d) Orderly — **Ans: (c) ambiguous**
43. He regards himself as a **patriot**. — (a) dutiful (b) traitor (c) nationalist (d) Loyal — **Ans: (b) traitor**
44. Indolent — (a) Unskilled (b) Sluggish (c) Lazy (d) Active — **Ans: (d) Active**
45. Developed countries are **at a disadvantage** to have access to modern science and technologies. — (a) privileged (b) useless (c) powered (d) Unhappy — **Ans: (a) privileged**
46. Traitor — People should vote for honest and loyal leaders not for betrayer and defender. — (a) Loyal (b) Honest (c) Betrayer (d) Defender — **Ans: (d) Defender**
47. She was upset seeing the **scattered** documents. — (a) Dissipated (b) Perished (c) Sprinkled (d) Gathered — **Ans: (d) Gathered**
48. Wax — (a) Cane (b) Wane (c) Wail (d) Polish — **Ans: (b) Wane**
49. Ranen feels that the **notion** of democracy is a farce. — (a) Reality (b) Law (c) Quotient (d) Portion — **Ans: (a) Reality**
50. Triumph — (a) Defeat (b) Attainment (c) Succeed (d) Capture — **Ans: (a) Defeat**
51. The patient found the doctor to be **obtuse** and distracted by constant calls on his phone. — (a) obese (b) banal (c) astute (d) Obsolete — **Ans: (c) astute**
52. His house was **filthy** with papers and eatables strewn everywhere. — (a) Integral (b) Dignified (c) Clean (d) Barren — **Ans: (c) Clean**

53. 'Sophisticated' — The tribe had no access to modern technology or resources and had to rely on primitive tools and techniques for survival. — (a) Modern (b) Techniques (c) Primitive (d) Technology — **Ans: (c) Primitive**
54. 'effervescent' — Sonali is bubbly and carefree in comparison to her sister Mitali's subdued nature. — (a) Bubbly (b) Comparison (c) Subdued (d) Carefree — **Ans: (c) Subdued**
55. He was **uncomfortable** at the sport event. — (a) Easeful (b) Difficult (c) Awkward (d) Troublesome — **Ans: (a) Easeful**
56. Innate — (a) Natural (b) Learned (c) Aquatic (d) Replete — **Ans: (b) Learned**
57. He was quite **violent** in his behaviour. — (a) Crazy (b) Gentle (c) Savage (d) Aggressive — **Ans: (b) Gentle**
58. Meticulous — (a) Heedful (b) Careless (c) Untidy (d) Careful — **Ans: (b) Careless**
59. Nervous — (a) Hesitant (b) Casual (c) Shaky (d) Courageous — **Ans: (d) Courageous**
60. It was **evident** from his gestures that he was feeling guilty. — (a) Profuse (b) Hidden (c) Correct (d) Visible — **Ans: (b) Hidden**
61. Hinder — (a) Aid (b) Monitor (c) Force (d) Domesticate — **Ans: (a) Aid**

62. 'Unsure' — Himanshi is preparing customised gifts and handicrafts for surprising Mehul on his retirement, but the date is not certain yet. — (a) date (b) handicrafts (c) customised (d) certain — **Ans: (d) certain**
63. Perseverance — (a) Eloquence (b) Irresolution (c) Readiness (d) Obstinacy — **Ans: (b) Irresolution**
64. He was **acquitted** of all the charges in the court today. — (a) Discharged (b) Conformed (c) Convicted (d) Praised — **Ans: (c) Convicted**
65. Her sudden **arrival** has disarranged my plans. — (a) Timing (b) Entrance (c) Departure (d) Appearance — **Ans: (c) Departure**
66. Her room looks very **messy** — (a) Organised (b) Chaotic (c) Untidy (d) Disordered — **Ans: (a) Organised**
67. There are ______ **(definite)** weaknesses in their security arrangements. — (a) subtle (b) vague (c) hazy (d) monotonous — **Ans: (b) vague**
68. This is such a **rowdy** classroom. — (a) Noisy (b) Compliant (c) Disorderly (d) Chattering — **Ans: (b) Compliant**
69. She will **probably** come tonight for a party. — (a) possibly (b) apparently (c) definitely (d) perhaps — **Ans: (c) definitely**
70. She was wearing a ______ new outfit. **(expensive)** — (a) costly (b) premium (c) precious (d) Cheap — **Ans: (d) Cheap**
71. Lament — (a) Deplore (b) Decry (c) Celebrate (d) Regret — **Ans: (c) Celebrate**
72. One should not **yell** at children. — (a) Terrorise (b) Notify (c) Whisper (d) Indemnify — **Ans: (c) Whisper**
73. Clear — (a) Clean (b) Opaque (c) Untidy (d) Mess — **Ans: (b) Opaque**
74. She is a **veteran** journalist known for her powerful writing style. — (a) Novice (b) Conditioned (c) Renowned (d) Creviced — **Ans: (a) Novice**
75. My energy **waned** to see my college team performing poorly in the match. — (a) Dimmed (b) Grew (c) Abated (d) Declined — **Ans: (b) Grew**
76. 'fetish' — Shilpi has a fixed indifference and obsession towards problems in life. — (a) Indifference (b) Obsession (c) Fixed (d) Problems — **Ans: (a) Indifference**
77. The apple tasted incredibly **sweet**. — (a) Delicious (b) Pleasant (c) Bitter (d) Special — **Ans: (c) Bitter**
78. The boy looked **disheveled** and lost. — (a) Tidy (b) Clumsy (c) Infuriated (d) Excited — **Ans: (a) Tidy**
79. I must have a chance to **rectify** my error. — (a) worsen (b) reason (c) modify (d) alter — **Ans: (a) worsen**
80. My **complaint** is that the gardener dozes off during work. — (a) Demur (b) Carp (c) Challenge (d) Praise — **Ans: (d) Praise**
81. The lizard's light brown skin acts as **camouflage** in the desert sand. — (a) vanishing (b) reveal (c) clothed (d) Mask — **Ans: (b) reveal**
82. Compulsory — (a) Obligatory (b) Subsidiary (c) Voluntary (d) Mandatory — **Ans: (c) Voluntary**

83. 'disenfranchise' — The parliament of Tonoco authorised the king to take over the reins of the government and disable the power of the council of ministers. — (a) Reins (b) Authorised (c) Powers (d) Disabled — **Ans: (b) Authorised**
84. Intentional — (a) Unplanned (b) Cordial (c) Amiable (d) Inanimate — **Ans: (a) Unplanned**
85. Prohibition — (a) Restriction (b) Inhibition (c) Permission (d) Direction — **Ans: (c) Permission**
86. They **praised** her for her bravery. — (a) Defended (b) Acclaimed (c) Assaulted (d) Rebuked — **Ans: (d) Rebuked**
87. I **confronted** the journalist with the evidence. — (a) Encountered (b) Resisted (c) Challenged (d) Avoided — **Ans: (d) Avoided**
88. Abandon — (a) Continue (b) Shorten (c) Quit (d) Surrender — **Ans: (c) Quit**
89. Selective — (a) Planned (b) Doubtful (c) Careless (d) Stimulating — **Ans: (c) Careless**
90. The crowd gave an **exultant** cheer to the football team when they were exiting the airport. — (a) disconsolate (b) rejoicing (c) jubilant (d) elated — **Ans: (a) disconsolate**
91. Memory — (a) Oblivion (b) Retrospection (c) Impart (d) Suppress — **Ans: (a) Oblivion**
92. Trivial — (a) Huge (b) Significant (c) Light (d) Heavy — **Ans: (b) Significant**
93. Conceal — (a) Reveal (b) Hide (c) Watch (d) See — **Ans: (a) Reveal**
94. The village fair was cancelled due to **incessant** rains. — (a) Daily (b) Continual (c) Insistent (d) Sporadic — **Ans: (d) Sporadic**
95. I **object** the false claims made against me. — (a) ease (b) restore (c) assent (d) ascertain — **Ans: (a) ease**
96. I don't like to work with people who are not **flexible**. — (a) Capable (b) Malleable (c) Rigid (d) Open-ended — **Ans: (c) Rigid**
97. Camaraderie — (a) Dislike (b) Amity (c) Companionship (d) Friendship — **Ans: (a) Dislike**
98. Ransack — (a) Seize (b) Raid (c) Protect (d) Pilfer — **Ans: (c) Protect**
99. The **colossal** building stood amidst the ruins bearing signs of Victorian architecture. — (a) Enormous (b) Extraordinary (c) Quaint (d) Tiny — **Ans: (d) Tiny**
100. The new Principal of the school is **stern**, yet he understands the needs of his subordinates. — (a) Flippant (b) Lenient (c) Desolate (d) Noxious — **Ans: (b) Lenient**
